reviews.review_text: It was almost as if we were an imposition  booking in. We questioned if we could view the available sites. Big problem!! As if to say why would you do that???|Terrible front desk service, clearly stated the hours are 9.00 am to 5.00pm yet at 9.3 0 am the next day the office still shows as closed. Let me say this receptionist didn’t really want to be there
Sanitaires très propres. Camping calme.

We stayed here for 3 nights and could not fault it, the bathroom and toilets were immaculate as were the grounds. Easy walk into town and also a good base if you are wanting to go into Hobart and other tourist places. Very quick and easy to get to these places from here.  Owners were very nice! Highly recommend, we will definately be back!

Pleasant, quiet stay.
Staff lovely , new management next month.
Managers were very nice and park is very clean. Great location to unhitch and visit Hobart and enjoy the lovely town of Richmond and surrounds- also close to Golf course and Zoo
Thoroughly enjoyed our short stay in our van here. Lovely & clean facilities. Had a great green area to camp in. Highly recommend.

Horrible people running this campsite. Travelling with a young family (including an 8mth old baby) and struggling to find potable water in Richmond, decided to pull in here to see if the park provided a ‘pay for water’ service. After saying they didn’t I proceeded to enquire about paying the $45 for a site for the night so that I could fill up our tanks and be on our merry way. The campground manager then enquired where we’d be heading and after finding out that we’d booked a nearby Farmstay that didn’t have potable water he quite aggressively stated “well that’s yours and the farmstays problem, I’m not going to let you book a site here just to fill your tanks”. Our experience in Tas to date has been a feeling that hospitality operators are very collaborative with other places (a lot of where we’ve travelled has been off the back of recommendations from friendly operators), this campsite operator however definitely has a chip on his shoulder about the likes of Hipcamp/Wikicamp destinations. Certainly did not look like a nice place for a young family to stay so if travelling with kids would give this place a miss.

The park itself is fine. Nice location. Very clean amenities.

Some (permanent?) residents in cabins made leaving/entering tricky with parking on the thoroughfare leaving a single vehicle lane out of a corner... but the staff were mixed.

I'd tried ringing (over multiple days) to book - never picked up. I also sent an email to book - no reply. I arrived hoping for a spot, while ringing on my 3hr drive there. Again, never picked up. Only to be told, rudely, they don't have time to look at emails (despite it listed on their website!!!).

I suggest you either need another admin staff member to deal with enquiries or use an online booking system... its 2026 after all. By using a booking system you won't get all the phone calls and emails, actually freeing up time to do other tasks.

An older male staff member was extremely rude and aggressive. He came to me in the morning to say that I had placed my tent in the wrong spot near a cabin. I apologized and explained that I had understood that the entire area was designated for tents.

He continued to scold me and then said, “Besides, you haven’t even paid.” I replied that I had paid and that he could not accuse me without checking first. He rudely asked for my name and then walked away without any apology.

I believe he behaved this way because I am traveling by bicycle, which I consider discriminatory. In any case, it was a very unpleasant experience, and this staff member deserves a serious warning for his unprofessional behavior.

Wir haben vor Abreise zwei Nächte hier verbracht. Die Umgebung ist sehr schön, der Service war gut und der Preis für einen Stellplatz mit Strom und Frischwasser auch gerechtfertigt.
Hervorzuheben sind die äußerst sauberen Sanitäranlagen + dass das duschen hier mit inklusive war. :)
Our room was fantastic.  Clean, comfortable and well appointed

Campground is good.
Game room exists but games arw aged and need replacement.
Pool is ok but not more.
Overall a bit expensive.
Powered site , yes we had a tent , we were put way down the back behind the pool shed . 80 mtrs from toilet block for $55 just not worth it.

Close to Richmond. Powered site F. $55pn.
Quiet location. Facilities are spotless. Staff are really friendly. As a result we've booked a second night.

We stayed one night on a powered site. The site was easily accessed and the amenities were very clean.
Richmond is a lovely town to visit. Would recommend.
This site is very local to the campervan pick up point at hobart airport.  It was very clean and tidy and the staff were helpful and a good place to start your tour around Tasmania.
Just had 4 nights here on a powered site. Was a great little park with good roomy sites. The amenities are very clean and the park is very well maintained. The town of Richmond is really lovely.
Nice place  but unfortunately not very welcoming or helpful at all
It is in the middle of winefield and goat farm. So beautiful. It is very close to the old town as well. The facility is bit old though.

We stayed here on a whim during our last night in Tasmania. Very happy with the clean and quiet powered sites. It was also a nice treat to be right next to a field with sheep and lambs in it, as we got to walk down and watch them for a while before dinner. While there I also saw the staff riding around tidying the place, so they clearly take pride in the caravan park.
Old park but clean
Stayed in a camper van. Good location,  quiet location all amenities highly clean
Good value caravan Park.
Nice place to park your  caravan . Good bath and toilet facilities. Host were lovely
If you’re a backpacker don’t even bother calling. Rude owner
Great, quiet park. Easy access. Close to Hobart and wineries, also scenic areas.

This is the 5th time to Tasmania for my mum and myself and we have stayed at this caravan park the last 3 times. We stay in a 2 bedroom cabin and they just have a really homely, comfortable feel about them. Both bedrooms have large comfy beds. Great heating in cabins. Very helpful service.
I unfortunately had to call an ambulance on the first night this trip at around 10.30pm due to a health issue I’m suffering with and the owners were very understanding about this.
Highly recommend this park, it’s a great place to stay while exploring surrounding areas.
Great cabin no36...bit of a walk to town..we drove..good staff would definitely stay again
What a lovely caravan park! Richmond is a totally gorgeous town and the wineries are fantastic!
Proximity to town is great. Very nice camp kitchen  Really nice facilities but the bathroom is not very central to all sites.
Very clean amenities and helpful friendly staff.

TERRIBLE Campsite -

We paid for 2 nights ($35 per night for the powered site).

On the first day , we were cleaning our van so we moved a lot of stuff outside on the grass. My partner was fixing our spice rack with a screw driver outside of our van. Soon after,  the site manager came angrily saying “This is not a build site! You can’t do this here. I tell you now. No mess here. Do you want to leave or not?”

We were very polite the whole time. Not sure what’s wrong with the manager.

Wouldn’t recommend this campsite to anyone. There are plenty of other cheap campsites in TAS with nicer people running them.
Great park to base yourself to explore the area. Very quiet. Good clean amenities and the pool was great for the grandkids. Very enjoyable.
We stayed in our own caravan.
Nice place to stay. The cabins are a good size for a few days. There's activities to do
Great place to stay and relax. Was a good base camp for adventuring around the area.
Beautiful area. Nice park. Staff super rude.

I wouldn’t recommend this campsite based on my experience. To provide some context, I’m generally an easygoing traveler and had the pleasure of meeting many kind and welcoming people during my time in Tasmania, including campsite managers and B&B hosts. However, my experience at this family-run campsite in Richmond was less than ideal. I found the atmosphere unwelcoming, particularly in interactions with the male host, who seemed difficult to approach or communicate with. For those seeking a smooth and enjoyable journey, it might be worth considering other options to avoid any potential discomfort.

Responding to the owner:

I have apologized countless times for my late arrival. When traveling to unfamiliar places, delays are sometimes unavoidable, and arriving late at a campsite should not be something unforgivable—especially when it was late at night, and I had a sleeping child with me. Yet, you treated us as though we were being expelled.
A friend who accompanied us was only there to ensure we arrived safely and could settle in for the night. We didn’t even unpack our luggage. I tried to explain the situation and resolve the matter with you, but you kept shouting, showing no willingness to communicate.
The next morning, I went to your campsite office again to apologize and explain the reasons for my late arrival the previous night. Instead of understanding, you continued shouting at me and told me to leave your campsite.
I have nothing more to add. Let others read the negative reviews—they are all truthful. No one is lying.

I wouldn’t recommend this campsite based on my experience. To provide some context, I’m generally an easygoing traveler and had the pleasure of meeting many kind and welcoming people during my time in Tasmania, including campsite managers and B&B hosts. However, my experience at this family-run campsite in Richmond was less than ideal. I found the atmosphere unwelcoming, particularly in interactions with the male host, who seemed difficult to approach or communicate with. For those seeking a smooth and enjoyable journey, it might be worth considering other options to avoid any potential discomfort.

2 night stay on powered site, ($50 per night)
14 minute walk to town fairly flat, be advised the town doesn’t kick off early so best option for coffee is the bakery
Clean amenities with shower on 6 minute timer, a quiet yet great location to Hobart (20mins) and coalvalley cellar doors, cheese factories along the way
Great stop ✋
Sites are easy to drive into. Friendly helpful staff at check in, very good amenities with great hot showers , close to town.

Booked in for a night in our motorhome. Good sized sites, great views, and friendly staff. Amenities are clean and well presented. Indoor heated pool, games room and outdoor play equipment for the kids, barbecue areas, and camp kitchens if needed as well.

Reasonable rates and a great location - we can't ask for much more!
Very clean and tidy Caravan park. Stayed for the night whilst travelling Tasmania.

NOT "pet friendly" as they advertise! Would not book us in because we have a German Shepherd, who is a beautiful, well behaved good girl. Total breed ignorance, as I know chihuahuas and other barking rats that are far more vicious than a trained Shepherd. If you are travelling with a large breed dog, go to Big4 Hobart Airport as they aren't as "doggist". Disappointed to say the least.
We stay here a lot. Beautiful little spot. 👌
Easy walk to town and wineries.

I booked 2 nights for 2  powered sites together at this caravan park for 2 campervans. My husband & I plus another couple - friends travelling with us. My original booking was made over 2 months ago. Then I rang the owner 24hours in advance of our arrival to ensure we definitely had 2 sites together for the 2 nights. I was told yes- no problem. Today when we arrived just before 2pm was told by caretaker couple ( not the owners) there was no room left. After much debate we had to go to Hobart airport caravan park as we had no choice. The opposite direction to our planned itinery. Can't believe the lack of responsibility- they just didn't care.

The park photo gales does not represent what the park looks like |Sahara desert is greener with more grass |One big  dust bowl. |Attitude of staff is not pleasant and very abrupt. |Rate of $50 per day is way over priced. |Will never stay here again and hope other people stay wide of this caravan park
Nice and clean amenities. Quite exposed to the weather and no view.
This is just out of town, walking distance. The sites are spacious and facilities are ok and the indoor swimming pool is a nice surprise. Good park

Travelled 400 kilometres to a preorganised camp site just to be told its not a party place and we are to young we are a couple with two young kids and two male friends aged 25 not happy with owners attitude we aint here to party with two young children came to see motocross thought it would of been a nice holiday but now nowhere to go!!!!!!

Stayed here 2 nights whilst travelling around Tassie on motorcycles. Was going to stay 1 night and move on. However Ted and family were so good stayed 2 nights. Great location highly recommended. Moto Guzzi club.
Had an excellent time
What an absolute delight staying at this immaculately clean park I didn’t want to leave, excellent service would highly recommend if travelling through!!

Great park!!
We have a bid rig, plenty of room for us to move around and park up. Great spot near the toilets and laundry. All amenities were lovely and clean. Showers work on a 6-minute shower. But it is plenty long enough for a shower.
The man who organised our site  was very helpful.
3 minute drive into the beautiful town of Richmond. 2 minute drive to Coal Valley golf course.
Fantastic location.

Lovely park and very friendly staff! The pool is lovely and heated! Though the showers have a 6 minute timer which turns the hot water off, so you have to wait 2 minutes in the cold to start it again. Be fast or you’ll get very cold!

Nice clean caravan park. We booked for 3 nights and ended up staying 5 as it was a good base to explore Hobart and surrounds without the hustle of more inner city campgrounds. Best sites where E,F,G,H along the fence.

Excellent quiet lovely park if on the other side to the packed caravan side where they are all packed in like sardines.|Quiet, tidy, and friendly. Staff are lovely.  Large caravan spaces and camp kitchens and facilities on this side as well.|Must make sure you book. Walking into town from the park if careful but disabilitity (wheelchair access) not safe . Side of the road is not wide and safe enough for about 100 metres from the park. From this section there is a very safefootpath into town.|

Nice clean and well maintained caravan park and amenities. Very quiet overnight.
Short walk to the township.

Friendly staff. We had the powered site for two nights. Location was clean and maintained well. |Clean water. There’s hot water in the shower (6mins) bbq works. There’s a half court in the compound.|The indoor pool was not available as the building was recently struck by lighting n that busted the heater system.

Friendly staff. We had the powered site for two nights.
Clean water. There’s hot water in the shower (6mins) bbq works

The camp kitchen were disgusting  could do with a good clean and the lady at reception  was actually  very  rude I would  not recommend  this park and if I was the big 4 group I would be  ashamed  to put my name to it
Friendly Park in Richmont and close to Hobart!
Really pretty park with very friendly staff and residents
Location is brilliant. Richmond in a lovely village and its only a short walk from the park. Amenities are old but well maintained and very clean.

Great park with clean amenities and nice views.
A bit more expensive than other parks.
